results.rendered_html: the daughter of Sūrya or the Sun (see  RV. i, 116, 17 ; also described as daughter of Prajāpati or of Savitṛ and wife of the Aśvins, and in other places as married to Soma; in  RV. i, 119, 2  she is called Ūrjānī, and in vi, 55, 4 , vi, 58, 4  the sister of Pūṣan [q.v.], who is described as loving her, and receiving her as a gift from the gods; accord. to some she represents a weak manifestation of the Sun; Sūryā Sāvitrī is regarded as the authoress of the Sūryā-sūkta,  RV. x, 85 ),  RV. ;  AV. ;  AitBr. ;  Kauś.
